claim: Words containing gallows glyphs (EVA t, p, k, f and bench variants cth, ckh, cph) occupy structurally distinct syntactic positions: they appear predominantly as the first content word after line-initial position and are followed by non-gallows words at a rate significantly higher than the corpus baseline, consistent with gallows functioning as topic-marking or noun-phrase-head indicators rather than phonemes.
evidence_for: H-BRADY-02 (confidence 0.7) establishes 'p' at 5.4x and 't' at 3.2x line-initial enrichment, already suggesting paragraph/section marking function
If gallows mark noun-phrase heads, they should be followed by modifier-class words (non-gallows) more often than preceded by them
The biological and herbal sections both show high gallows density, consistent with noun-phrase headings for plant/anatomy entries
evidence_against: Bench gallows (cth/ckh/cph) appear at <0.5x line-initial rate, suggesting they do not share the paragraph-marker function and may be phonemic

If gallows are purely positional markers, the within-line bigram prediction (gallows followed by non-gallows) may be confounded by the general rarity of gallows words making them structurally salient by frequency alone
test: For all within-line word bigrams (w_i, w_{i+1}): compute the conditional probability P(w_{i+1} is non-gallows | w_i is gallows) and compare to the marginal P(w is non-gallows) in the corpus. Separately compute P(w_{i+1} is gallows | w_i is gallows). Run chi-squared test with 1 degree of freedom. Stratify by plain gallows (t,p,k,f) vs. bench gallows (cth,ckh,cph) vs. no gallows.
test_metric: P(non-gallows follows gallows) vs. corpus baseline; chi-squared statistic and p-value; transition matrix for gallowsâ†’gallows, gallowsâ†’non-gallows, non-gallowsâ†’gallows, non-gallowsâ†’non-gallows
test_threshold: Confirm syntactic-marker hypothesis if P(non-gallows | gallows) > corpus baseline by >15 percentage points AND chi-squared p < 0.001 for plain gallows; deny if plain and bench gallows show indistinguishable transition profiles
tests_remaining: For all within-line word bigrams (w_i, w_{i+1}): compute the conditional probability P(w_{i+1} is non-gallows | w_i is gallows) and compare to the marginal P(w is non-gallows) in the corpus. Separately compute P(w_{i+1} is gallows | w_i is gallows). Run chi-squared test with 1 degree of freedom. Stratify by plain gallows (t,p,k,f) vs. bench gallows (cth,ckh,cph) vs. no gallows.
reasoning: If gallows glyphs are structural markers, any mapping that treats them as phonemes will systematically misalign decoded text; confirming this collapses a large class of cipher hypotheses and constrains any decipherment to treat gallows as non-phonemic structural tokens
